stories.title: Create Environment
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can create a named environment via the UI
- Environment names must be non-empty
- Multiple environments can exist simultaneously
- User can rename and delete environments
- Environments persist across app restarts

Technical Approach:
- Add `environments` table: `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, created_at DATETIME`
- Add migration in `db/db.go`
- Add `db/environments.go` with CreateEnvironment, ListEnvironments, RenameEnvironment, DeleteEnvironment functions
- Expose on `App` struct in `app.go`: `CreateEnvironment(name string)`, `ListEnvironments()`, `RenameEnvironment(id, name)`, `DeleteEnvironment(id)`
- Frontend: new `environmentsStore.ts` in `frontend/src/store/` with actions mirroring Go bindings
- Frontend: EnvironmentPanel component or modal to list/add/rename/delete environments
- Regenerate Wails bindings after adding App methods

Files to touch:
- db/db.go (schema migration)
- db/environments.go (new file)
- app.go
- frontend/src/store/environmentsStore.ts (new file)
- frontend/src/components/EnvironmentPanel.tsx (new file)
- frontend/src/App.tsx (integrate panel)
- frontend/src/wailsjs/go/ (regenerated)

Tests:
- Go: `db/environments_test.go` — TestCreateEnvironment, TestListEnvironments, TestRenameEnvironment, TestDeleteEnvironment (use in-memory SQLite)
- Vitest: `frontend/src/store/environmentsStore.test.ts` — mock Wails bindings, test createEnvironment action updates state

stories.title: Add Environment Variables
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can add key-value variable pairs to an environment
- User can edit existing variables
- User can delete variables
- Variables persist across app restarts
- An environment can have zero or more variables

Technical Approach:
- Add `environment_variables` table: `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, environment_id INTEGER REFERENCES environments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, key TEXT NOT NULL, value TEXT NOT NULL`
- Add migration in `db/db.go`
- Add CRUD functions in `db/environments.go`: SetVariable(envID, key, value), GetVariables(envID), DeleteVariable(id)
- Expose on `App` struct: `SetVariable`, `GetVariables`, `DeleteVariable`
- Frontend: VariableEditor component (reuse or extend KeyValueTable pattern) rendered inside EnvironmentPanel
- Store: extend `environmentsStore` with variable state and actions

Files to touch:
- db/db.go (schema migration)
- db/environments.go
- app.go
- frontend/src/store/environmentsStore.ts
- frontend/src/components/EnvironmentPanel.tsx
- frontend/src/wailsjs/go/ (regenerated)

Tests:
- Go: extend `db/environments_test.go` — TestSetVariable, TestGetVariables, TestDeleteVariable, TestCascadeDeleteOnEnvironmentDelete
- Vitest: extend `environmentsStore.test.ts` — test setVariable, deleteVariable actions update local state correctly

stories.title: Select Active Environment
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can select one environment as active from a dropdown in the top bar
- Active environment selection persists across app restarts
- Selecting 'None' disables variable substitution
- Only one environment can be active at a time

Technical Approach:
- Add `settings` table (or reuse if exists): `key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT` — store `active_environment_id`
- Add `db/settings.go` with GetSetting(key) and SetSetting(key, value)
- Expose on `App` struct: `GetActiveEnvironment()`, `SetActiveEnvironment(id int)`
- Frontend: EnvironmentSelector dropdown component in top bar (App.tsx or a TopBar component)
- Store: `environmentsStore` holds `activeEnvironmentId` state; `setActiveEnvironment` action calls Go binding and updates state

Files to touch:
- db/db.go (settings table migration)
- db/settings.go (new file)
- app.go
- frontend/src/store/environmentsStore.ts
- frontend/src/components/EnvironmentSelector.tsx (new file)
- frontend/src/App.tsx
- frontend/src/wailsjs/go/ (regenerated)

Tests:
- Go: `db/settings_test.go` — TestGetSetting_Default, TestSetAndGetActiveEnvironment
- Vitest: test setActiveEnvironment updates activeEnvironmentId in store; test selector renders correct active environment

stories.title: Use Variables in Requests
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can type {{variable_name}} in URL, header values, query parameter values, and body fields
- The UI renders the raw text including {{ }} without escaping or error
- No validation error is shown for {{}} syntax in any request field
- Variables are not resolved in the editor — only at send time (US-5)

Technical Approach:
- Audit RequestEditor, KeyValueTable, BodyEditor components to ensure {{ }} characters are not stripped or escaped
- No backend changes required for this story
- Ensure form inputs are plain text inputs that accept any character
- Add a visual hint (e.g. tooltip or placeholder) showing {{var_name}} example in URL field

Files to touch:
- frontend/src/components/RequestEditor.tsx
- frontend/src/components/KeyValueTable.tsx
- frontend/src/components/BodyEditor.tsx

Tests:
- Vitest: render RequestEditor, type '{{base_url}}/users' into URL field, assert value is preserved exactly
- Vitest: render KeyValueTable, enter '{{api_key}}' as a header value, assert no transformation occurs

stories.title: Variable Interpolation
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- When the user sends a request, all {{variable_name}} tokens in URL, headers, params, and body are replaced with the corresponding value from the active environment
- If a variable is not found in the active environment, the token is left as-is (no error, no crash)
- If no environment is active, no interpolation occurs
- Interpolation happens server-side (Go) before the HTTP request is dispatched
- Resolved values are NOT stored back to the DB — interpolation is ephemeral

Technical Approach:
- Add `interpolator.go` (or add to `httpclient/client.go`): func Interpolate(template string, vars map[string]string) string — replace all {{key}} occurrences using the vars map
- Modify `App.SendRequest` (or equivalent in app.go): before calling httpclient, fetch active environment variables, build map[string]string, call Interpolate on URL, each header value, each param value, and body
- Interpolation regex: `\{\{([^}]+)\}\}` — capture group 1 is the key

Files to touch:
- interpolator.go (new file, package main or httpclient)
- app.go (SendRequest method)
- httpclient/client.go (if interpolation is passed in as resolved fields)

Tests:
- Go: `interpolator_test.go` — TestInterpolate_SingleVar, TestInterpolate_MultipleVars, TestInterpolate_MissingKeyLeftAsIs, TestInterpolate_NoActiveEnvironment, TestInterpolate_SpecialCharsInValue, TestInterpolate_EmptyTemplate
- Go integration: TestSendRequest_InterpolatesURL — use httptest.NewServer, set active env with base_url variable, send request with {{base_url}}/ping, assert correct URL was called
